bird.name: Ridgway's rail
bird.summary: Ridgway's rail (Rallus obsoletus) is a species of bird found principally along the Pacific coast of North America from the San Francisco Ba…

relations.subject: Ridgway's rail
relations.evidence: A member of the rail family, Rallidae, it is a chicken-sized bird that lives in brackish tidal marshes and rarely flies
relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。

relations.subject: Ridgway's rail
relations.evidence: Ridgway's rail has been observed to forage in or near relatively disturbed areas, leading one to deduce the importance of protecting even numeral marsh areas; for example this species was seen foraging in a small mudflat area within Seal Slough in San Mateo, three miles from the nearest known breeding area in Belmont.T.E
relations.object_summary: 具有较高含水量和密生植被的浅水环境。
